lede: A look at July 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Daniela Avanzini and Fedi Nuril, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2008 riot in Mongolia
events.desc: Riots erupt in Mongolia in response to allegations of fraud surrounding the 2008 legislative elections.

events.title: North Korea
events.desc: A North Korean Ilyushin Il-62M jet en route to Conakry Airport in Guinea crashes into the Fouta Djallon mountains in Guinea-Bissau, killing all 23 people on board.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: start of the First Battle of El Alamein.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Russia launches an offensive against Austria-Hungary to capture Galicia, its final offensive of the war.

events.title: Sholes and Glidden typewriter
events.desc: The Sholes and Glidden typewriter, the first commercially successful typewriter, goes on sale.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Battle of Malvern Hill takes place. It is the last of the Seven Days Battles, part of George B. McClellan's Peninsula Campaign.

events.title: Thomas Lempriere
events.desc: Thomas Lempriere and James Clark Ross carve a marker on the Isle of the Dead in Van Diemen's Land to measure tidal variations, one of the earliest surviving benchmarks for sea level rise.

events.title: Westminster Assembly
events.desc: First meeting of the Westminster Assembly, a council of theologians ("divines") and members of the Parliament of England appointed to restructure the Church of England, at Westminster Abbey in London.

events.title: Jan van Essen and Hendrik Vos
events.desc: Jan van Essen and Hendrik Vos become the first Lutheran martyrs, burned at the stake by Roman Catholic authorities in Brussels.

deaths.text: Alex Delvecchio, Canadian ice hockey player and coach
Peter E. Gillquist, American priest and author
Jurriaan Schrofer, Dutch sculptor, designer and educator
Eliel Saarinen, Finnish-American architect, co-designed the National Museum of Finland
